headline: XLRE Hits 11% Gain This Year; Can the Payout Stay Safe
summary: The Real Estate Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLRE) is the cheapest way to own the REIT slice of the S&P 500, with an expense ratio of just 8 basis points and a portfolio that pays out income four times a year. headline: How Senior Housing and Warehouses Defend Monthly Payouts Today
summary: The First Trust S&P REIT Index Fund (NYSEARCA:FRI) gives investors exposure to American commercial real estate cash flow without picking between malls, warehouses, and senior living towers. Because REITs must distribute at least 90% of taxable income, FRI’s payout rises and falls with rent collected by underlying companies. The question for income investors is whether ... How Senior Housing and Warehouses Defend Monthly Payouts Today

headline: Is It Time To Reconsider Welltower (WELL) After Strong Multi Year Rally?
summary: Wondering if Welltower at US$205.33 is offering fair value or a stretched price? This article walks through the key valuation angles so you can judge for yourself. The stock is up 9.8% year to date and 35.1% over the past year, although it has recently fallen 5.0% over the last week and 3.2% over the past month. Recent headlines have focused on Welltower's position as a major health care real estate owner, with investors watching how sector sentiment and capital flows affect large REITs...
